title: Optimistic Updates
description:
Implement optimistic UI updates for a todo list where changes appear instantly and roll back on failure.

### Requirements

- Toggling a todo's completed state updates the UI immediately (optimistic)
- A PATCH request is sent to the API in the background
- If the API request fails, the UI rolls back to the previous state
- Show a brief error toast on rollback
- Deleting a todo also works optimistically with rollback
- During the API call, show a subtle sync indicator on the affected item
- Disable rapid re-toggling while a request is in flight for that item

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Todo item | `todo-{id}` |
| Toggle checkbox | `toggle-{id}` |
| Delete button | `delete-{id}` |
| Sync indicator | `syncing-{id}` |
| Error toast | `error-toast` |
| Todo list | `todo-list` |
constraints:
- UI must update before the API response arrives
- Failed requests must roll back the UI
- Must show a brief error message on failure
- Items with in-flight requests should be visually indicated
- No double-toggling while syncing
hints: Save the previous state before applying the optimistic update. If the API call fails, restore the saved state.
Track which item IDs are currently syncing in a Set. Disable interactions for those items.
Use a try/catch around the fetch. In the catch block, restore the previous state and show the error toast.
